title: dalbergia hostilis
intro: Dalbergia hostilis, also known as Anwala or Indian ink tree, is a plant species native to the Western Ghats of India, known for its dark wood and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Dalbergia hostilis provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic. The plant is traditionally prepared into herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
